description: By setting a `dir` in the faillock configuration account lockouts will persist across reboots.
rationale:
Having lockouts persist across reboots ensures that account is only unlocked by an administrator.
If the lockouts did not persist across reboots an attack could simply reboot the system to continue brute force attacks against the accounts on the system.
ocil_clause: the "dir" option is not set to a non-default documented tally log directory, is missing or commented out
ocil:
Verify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file is configured use a non-default faillock directory to ensure contents persist after reboot:

$ sudo grep 'dir =' /etc/security/faillock.conf

dir = /var/log/faillock
fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 maintain the contents of the faillock directory after a reboot.

Add/Modify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file to match the following line:

dir = /var/log/faillock
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must ensure account lockouts persist.

warnings.general: This rule is deprecated in favor of the accounts_passwords_pam_faillock_dir rule.Please consider replacing this rule in your files as it is not expected to receive updates as of version 0.1.65.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must ensure account lockouts persist.
stig.vuldiscussion:
Having lockouts persist across reboots ensures that account is only unlocked by an administrator.
If the lockouts did not persist across reboots an attack could simply reboot the system to continue brute force attacks against the accounts on the system.
stig.checktext:
Verify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file is configured use a non-default faillock directory to ensure contents persist after reboot with the following command:

$ grep 'dir =' /etc/security/faillock.conf

dir = /var/log/faillock

If the "dir" option is not set to a non-default documented tally log directory, is missing or commented out, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 maintain the contents of the faillock directory after a reboot.

Add/Modify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file to match the following line:

dir = /var/log/faillock

title: Account Lockouts Must Persist
